arts.ttl: Barcelona plotting move for highly sought-after Newcastle star
arts.sum: Barcelona are keen on signing a new striker as a long-term replacement for Robert Lewandowski and have identified Alexander Isak as one of their primary targets if they fail to secure Manchester City star Erling Haaland. The La Liga giants are also considering Liverpool’s Darwin Nunez as an alternative option. Recent reports have stated that […] The post Barcelona plotting move for highly sought-after Newcastle star appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: 'We don't hide', Newcastle United sent attacking pledge by 31-year-old manager
arts.sum: Speaking ahead of the trip to Tyneside, Hurzeler said: "We want to achieve great things and I think Newcastle is a big club and they want to achieve great things. The 31-year-old head coach is daring to win at St James' Park in what will be his first visit to the stadium and he brings his Brighton team here on the back of an excellent result against Tottenham Hotspur.

arts.ttl: Triple test Newcastle United cannot fail even at this stage of the season
arts.sum: Out of the blue on a whimsy autumn night when cold begins to bite and warm feelings are recalled, my mind drifted back to the great old days in a great city highlighted for me by Cochran's original rendition of Three Steps to Heaven which was a personal favourite, and it dawned on me that my other great love, Newcastle United, are about to embark on three significant steps of their own towards what could become Premier League heaven. I vividly remember rocking up to an Eddie Cochran concert at the Newcastle Empire in March of 1960 when he was supported by the legendary likes of Gene Vincent, Billy Fury, Joe Brown and Georgie Fame which was quite a bill.

arts.ttl: The key 11-day period that could make or break Newcastle United's season
arts.sum: Newcastle United boss Eddie Howe afforded himself a laugh back in late August when he was asked if the week ahead would be "pivotal" as far as the Magpies' season was concerned. So staying in touch now is vital for Howe's team and three games in 11 days before the month is out can see Newcastle shape their own campaign by beating Brighton, getting something at Chelsea in the Premier League and then booking a slot in the quarter-finals of the Carabao Cup.

arts.ttl: Matt O'Riley closes in on Brighton injury return as former Celtic goal hero joins English minnows - Parkhead bulletin
arts.sum: O'Riley's debut for the Seagulls was cut short after just eight minutes back in August.

arts.ttl: Chelsea destroyed and comeback drama as Newcastle's superb 15-5 record on line
arts.sum: Eddie Howe only used one substitute in this game and rested both Isak and Sandro Tonali, perhaps with the Champions League tie against AC Milan in mind, three days later. Fans will be hoping they can hit their stride following the international break with the brand of football which has become their hallmark under Eddie Howe.

arts.ttl: Gary Neville theory emerges after Eddie Howe verdict draws sharp response from Newcastle fans
arts.sum: With Newcastle performing better than Manchester United this season, some readers have suggested Neville wanted Howe to be England boss in an attempt to influence the Toon's push for European football. Newcastle United supporters have been quick to point out a theory as to why Gary Neville was so keen for Eddie Howe to be appointed as England manager.
